failure_modes.text: Teams can mistake the noisiest queue or highest-status pain point for the real constraint.
failure_modes.mitigation: Trace the flow end to end and measure where work-in-progress actually accumulates; the real constraint is where throughput is physically limited, not where complaints are loudest.

failure_modes.text: Parallelizing around a gating dependency can make the roadmap look busy while throughput stays pinned.
failure_modes.mitigation: Subordinate all other work to the constraint: do not optimize non-bottleneck steps until the gating step is resolved or its capacity is expanded.

failure_modes.text: Availability or substitution bias can push the team toward a familiar frame instead of the true system limiter.
failure_modes.mitigation: Test multiple frames against the throughput data; if a different model identifies a different constraint, the first diagnosis was biased by familiarity.

premortem_questions.text: Which step would still throttle the whole system if I funded every other improvement on the list?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Which step would still throttle the whole system if I funded every other improvement on the list?

premortem_questions.text: Is the worst case bad enough?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Is the worst case bad enough?

premortem_questions.text: How does what you're doing solve the problem, and how does it advance your thinking?
premortem_questions.source_quote: How does what you’re doing solve the problem? and How does it advance your thinking?

heuristics.text: Find the key driver instead of optimizing every visible pain point.
Use the Pareto principle to concentrate resources on the few inputs that unlock most throughput.
Manage cognitive load by chunking and scaffolding when the bottleneck is working-memory capacity.

curation_notes.summary: The raw file is especially strong on throughput misdiagnosis and queue-focused pre-mortem language, so the chunk set stays close to those interventions.
donor_drops: Skipped donor routing overlays because they add runtime flavor without improving intervention-semantic fidelity.
open_questions: The cognitive-load heuristic is slightly broader than the operational queue lane, but the raw file clearly treats it as part of the bottleneck playbook.

allies: The raw allies section is strong on cognitive load theory, Feynman technique, Minto structure, and systemic synthesis.
antagonists: The raw antagonists section is useful future relation material around fast thinking and Maslow's hammer.
structured_tensions: The source includes explicit structured tensions against optimization-theory, opportunity-cost, and confirmation-bias.
